name: Solid wood entry door (no glazing)
note: Common pre-1990s. Below IECC opaque-door U-0.32 max for new construction.

name: Insulated steel entry door (foam core)
note: Polyurethane core gives U~0.20 for slab; whole-door including frame ~0.28. Meets MA Stretch.

name: Insulated fiberglass entry door (PU foam core)
note: Best-in-class opaque door U. Therma-Tru/Andersen typical. Meets MA Specialized.

name: Insulated steel door with 1/4-lite double-pane Low-E glazing
note: Adding glazing raises U; ratio per glazing area. NFRC label is whole-door.

name: French (in-swing) fiberglass with full Low-E argon glazing
note: Full-glazed door behaves more like a window for energy.

name: Storm door (aluminum frame Low-E glass) over primary
note: Storm door alone is not the energy boundary; improves whole-assembly when paired with primary door.
